violations.text: Per PIC, pasta with meat sauce was received at 116 DF from Alvarez high school. Food was observed between 104-112 DF in the hot hold unit at the time of inspection. Food was picked up to be reheated at high school. Food was brought back at 165 DF. Establishment is to send hot food that is delivered below 135 DF and cold food that is delivered higher than 41 DF back to mother kitchen. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 09/10/2025
The non-food contact surfaces of the ice chest and milk cooler have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, ice, and/or other debris. New Violation. Correct By: 03/27/2025

Salads were observed held at 54-57 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Per interview with PIC, salads were just put in coolers for field trip, PIC added more ice packs at time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 10/24/2024

The door to the outside, located in the cafeteria, is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 05/16/2024
The establishment does not have a written procedure for cleaning vomit and diarrhea. Inspector provided guidance at time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/22/2024

The door to the outside, located in the cafeteria, is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 05/06/2024
Ceiling in kitchen net to light fixture is in poor repair. Inspector observed peeling and cracked paint. New Violation. Correct By: 05/06/2024

The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety at all times when time temperature control for safety (TCS) food is being prepared, handled, and/or served. New Violation. Correct By: 05/08/2023

Milk was observed held at 47-48degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Product disposed New Violation. Correct By: 05/08/2023
Food product and single service items were observed stored on the floor in the storage area. Food must be stored at least 6 inches above the floor. New Violation. Correct By: 05/12/2023

The floors in the kitchen and storage area has an accumulation of soil residue and food debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 05/12/2023
